task_completion.description: Agent preserved the original names of the exercises without renaming them to the acronyms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main 'Name' field in the edit forms or the primary titles on the exercise pages still show 'Romanian Deadlift', 'Overhead Press', and 'Bulgarian Split Squat', and have not been replaced by 'RDL', 'OHP', or 'BSS'.

integrity.description: Agent used the wger web interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the web browser with the wger application loaded and interacted with, rather than terminal commands, API calls, or database scripts.

integrity.description: Agent modified existing exercises rather than creating new ones.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shows editing existing exercises (e.g., 'Edit exercise' heading or modifying an existing entry) rather than 'Add new exercise' forms being submitted with the acronyms.

integrity.description: Agent actually typed or entered the aliases into the correct fields.
integrity.visual_evidence: The aliases appear in the designated 'Aliases' form fields or proper UI elements within the wger application, not just pasted into a random text editor or search bar.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires adding specific aliases to three existing exercises in the wger fitness manager: 'RDL' to 'Romanian Deadlift', 'OHP' to 'Overhead Press', and 'BSS' to 'Bulgarian Split Squat'. The original exercise names must remain unchanged, and no new exercises should be created.
